subject.title: Mathematics Analysis and Approaches (AA)
subject.description:
**IB Mathematics Analysis and Approaches** is a **Group 5** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **number and algebra, functions, geometry and trigonometry, statistics and probability, and calculus**, with a strong focus on **theoretical understanding, formal mathematical reasoning, and analytical problem-solving**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2021**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Non-calculator written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 2** | Calculator allowed written exam | 90 minutes | 120 minutes | 40% |
| **Paper 3** | Calculator allowed problem-solving | N/A | 60 minutes | 20% (HL only) |
| **Mathematical Exploration** | Internal Assessment | 20 hours | 20 hours | 20% |

strengths: Headings, numbered subsections and tables give a clear overall structure (intro/body/conclusion).
Logical development is solid, with each section building on the previous (CLT theory â calculations â graphs â comparison â conclusion).
The research question is succinctly stated and tables (e.g. Table 1) effectively present computed statistics, aiding comprehension.
weaknesses: Pagination fragments and inconsistent figure cross-referencing reduce polish and clarity.
Noticeable repetition of material (e.g. restatements of the 68-95-99.7 rule) affects conciseness.
Missing axis labels and legends in plots and leftover placeholder symbols ("\overline{a}") detract from professionalism.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Strand assessments place coherence and structure at band 3 (good logical flow and organization) but conciseness at band 2 (some irrelevant repetition). Balancing these, a score of 3/4 accurately reflects strong overall presentation with room to improve focus and polishing.

strengths: Correct use of standard notation for mean, variance and Ï in many equations.
Effective multiple representations such as tables, bar charts and overlaid density curves support analysis.
Clear Gaussian curve illustration and succinct interval notation reinforce understanding.
weaknesses: Frequent formatting errors (missing subscripts, stray LaTeX tokens, undefined â\overline{a}â) hinder readability.
Explanatory prose and derivations are occasionally sketchy (e.g. missing variance proof for discrete uniform).
Incomplete transitions (e.g. from discrete spikes to continuous density) and dense paragraphs reduce clarity; code snippets lack context.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Mathematical Communication strands average around band 2 (some appropriate notation, some representations, some logical clarity) with occasional reach into band 3. A 2/4 reflects these consistent but error-prone communications warranting clearer, more rigorous presentation.

strengths: The explorationâs personal anecdote demonstrates genuine independent thinking and clear engagement.
Choice of comparing 6- and 12-sided dice via bespoke Python plots shows initiative and a personal approach.
Designing a custom sequence of n values highlights thoughtful engagement with the topic.
weaknesses: Methods and analysis remain within standard textbook approaches rather than highly innovative.
The personal approach, while clear, lacks deeper theoretical novelty or unique methodology.
Creativity present but not exceptional; more originality in modelling or questions could elevate this further.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All three strands sit at band 2 (significant independent thinking, clear personal approach, good creativity). Hence a score of 2/3 aptly reflects solid but not outstanding personal engagement.

strengths: Basic qualitative comparisons (e.g. dice with wider faces yield flatter curves) and confirmation of the CLT show minimal reflection.
The student notes how mean and variance scale with dice size, offering a basic evaluation of outcomes.
weaknesses: Reflection is limited in depth, lacking discussion of model errors or theoretical versus simulated discrepancies.
Critical analysis is superficial, with no probing of limitations, alternative interpretations or extensions.
Evaluation of outcomes stops at basic remarks without exploring implications or future directions.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: All Reflection strands are assessed at band 1 (limited depth, basic analysis, limited evaluation). A score of 1/3 accurately represents minimal reflective engagement needing more critical insight and extension.

strengths: All mathematics used (means, variances, CLT, z-scores) is directly relevant and correctly applied to the research question.
Computations and graphs align with derived parameters, demonstrating good understanding with only minor errors.
Inclusion of the standardization formula and clear Z-score examples shows thorough grasp of SL techniques.
Comprehensive tables (e.g. side-by-side dice comparison) enhance depth and clarity of analysis.
weaknesses: Techniques remain within standard SL syllabus without proofs (e.g. variance formula derivation missing).
Minor algebraic typos (e.g. occasional missing minus signs) slightly detract from full accuracy.
Level of mathematics is appropriate but not yet at âexpertâ or beyond-syllabus sophistication.
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Relevance is strong (band 3), level appropriateness is largely at course level (band 2) and understanding is good with minor errors (band 4). Given the SL weighting and overall solid use of mathematics, a 4/6 fairly distributes the examinerâs total while reflecting both strengths and areas to deepen mathematical rigour.

strandSelections.reasoning: Arguments are generally understandable, but some derivations are sketchy (e.g. variance formula for discrete uniform not proven) and explanatory prose is occasionally disjointed.  Therefore the logical flow/clarity is âsomeâ, not âgoodâ.

Computations of means, variances and intervals are numerically correct; graphs align with derived parameters.  Minor algebraic typos (e.g. missing minus signs) exist but do not materially affect results, indicating good understanding with only minor errors.

The student employs formulae, tables, bar charts and multiple overlayed density curves, demonstrating effective use of more than one representation to support ideas.  Minor labelling issues prevent a top band.

The exploration follows a logical sequence (Introduction â CLT theory â calculations â graphs â comparison â conclusion).  Each section builds on the previous one and there are no major jumps in reasoning.  Minor lapses such as repeated explanations of the CLT and occasional digressions prevent it reaching the âexcellentâ descriptor, but overall coherence is solid.
Creating Python-style plots and interval tables indicates good initiative and some creativity, though techniques remain standard; hence the middle band fits best.

The student offers basic comparisons (means roughly double, variances quadruple) but stops short of deeper analytical discussion (e.g. goodness-of-fit measures).  This is âbasicâ rather than âthoughtfulâ analysis.

Evaluation is confined to qualitative remarks about predictability and variability; there is no consideration of model errors, simulation vs. theoretical results, or possible extensions.  Consequently evaluation is âlimitedâ.

Reflection is limited to restating that results âconfirm the CLTâ and that wider dice yield flatter curves; there is little probing of limitations or alternative interpretations.  Depth therefore remains âlimitedâ.

Standard notation for mean, variance and Ï is used, but there are frequent formatting errors (missing subscripts, stray LaTeX tokens, â\overline{a}â).  Hence usage is only âsome appropriateâ, not mostly consistent.
The exploration links the topic to personal childhood interest and designs a bespoke sequence of n values.  This evidences a clear personal approach, but not an âinnovativeâ one.

While most material is relevant, there is noticeable repetition (e.g. multiple restatements of the 68-95-99 rule) and placeholder symbols â\overline{a}â remain in several formulas.  This exceeds âminimalâ irrelevance but is not yet âconcise throughoutâ.

All mathematics (mean, variance, CLT, z-scores) is relevant and applied correctly to the stated question.  Application is appropriate but not yet âeffectiveâ in the sense of sophisticated modelling or rigorous proofs, placing it in band 3.
Choice of comparing 6- and 12-sided dice and producing own code-generated graphs shows significant independent thinking, though grounded in textbook methods rather than novel theory.

Headings, numbered subsections, tables and graphs give the report a clear structure (intro/body/conclusion).  Pagination fragments are distracting and cross-referencing of figures is inconsistent, so the organisation is good rather than exceptional.

Techniques are almost entirely at SL level (basic probability, descriptive stats, normal pdf).  There is no material clearly beyond syllabus, so the work is âmostly at course levelâ, matching band 2.

annotations.comment: Consider streamlining the introductory section by reducing minor digressions and focusing on defining key terms to improve conciseness and relevance.
The research question is stated succinctly and establishes clear scope, enhancing coherence and guiding the investigation effectively.
The bibliographic footnotes lack a consistent format. Adopt a single citation style (e.g., APA) and apply throughout.
Equations for density functions are clearly notated with proper use of sigma and mu, demonstrating accurate mathematical communication.
The personal anecdote effectively demonstrates genuine interest, providing evidence of independent thinking and personal engagement.
The description of the Central Limit Theorem ends abruptly. Completing sentences and clarifying assumptions would strengthen the theoretical foundation.
The placeholder â\overline{a}â appears in several formulas without definition. Explicitly define all notation to avoid confusion.
The variance formula for a discrete uniform distribution is stated without proof. A brief derivation or citation would strengthen mathematical rigor.
Stray footnote markers within formulas distract from clarity. Ensure equations are cleanly presented and footnotes placed separately.
Table 1 concisely presents computed means, variances, and standard deviations. This effective representation aids comprehension.
Cross-referencing of figures is inconsistent. Use consistent labels (e.g., âFigure 1â) and refer to them in the text for clarity.
Some paragraphs reiterate basic Gaussian properties at length. Trimming repetitive descriptions will improve conciseness and focus.
The Gaussian curve illustration is well drawn and labeled, reinforcing theoretical discussion with strong visual support.
Axis labels and legends are missing in the density plot, reducing interpretability. Add descriptive axes and legends.
The narrative describing discrete spikes lacks a formal transition to continuous approximation. Clarify how discrete sums converge to continuous density.
The description of the 68-95-99.7 rule mixes languages and formatting. Consistently use English and correct mathematical notation.
The interval notation is presented succinctly and reinforces understanding of concentration of probabilities.
Placeholders such as â\overline{a}â appear in listings for 12-sided dice. Ensure all symbols are defined or removed.
The concentration comparison figure effectively visualizes 6- vs 12-sided dice, supporting analytical discussion.
The sample concentration calculation paragraph is dense and hard to parse. Consider breaking it into bullet points or equations for clarity.
The standardization formula is presented accurately. Including this formal definition highlights strong use of SL mathematical techniques.
One bullet point in the Z_n list appears incomplete and duplicates content. Review list items for accuracy and consistency.
The Z-score example for n=10 is clear and illustrates how extreme values relate to the normal range.
The embedded code snippet lacks context or language annotation. Format code blocks and indicate the programming environment for clarity.
Table 6 provides a comprehensive side-by-side comparison of both dice types. This enhances depth and clarity of analysis.
